title: astragalus macrostephanus
intro: Astragalus macrostephanus, also known as the Mongolian milkvetch, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of China and Mongolia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties, which enable it to help the body adapt to stress and modulate the immune system. The medicinal properties of Astragalus macrostephanus are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-viral effects, providing various health benefits such as enhanced immune function, reduced inflammation, and improved cardiovascular health. Astragalus macrostephanus is typically prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall well-being.
intro_study: According to "Biological & pharmaceutical bulletin", astragalus macrostephanus may have a role in inhibiting bacterial infection-associated preterm labor by suppressing the productions of IL-6, PGE2, and LTC4 by human amnion cells.
